summary: Tested Outlook/Microsoft 365 Calendar integration alongside existing Google Calendar. All backend APIs working correctly. Frontend Integrations page displays both Google and Outlook cards with Connect buttons. AppointmentDetail page shows ICS button; Google/Outlook sync buttons correctly hidden when user has no calendar connections. Multi-provider sync status API returns correct format {google: {...}, outlook: {...}}.
passed_tests: A. GET /api/calendar/connections - Returns empty list for user with no connections (PASSED)
B. GET /api/calendar/connect/google - Returns valid Google OAuth authorization_url (PASSED)
C. GET /api/calendar/connect/outlook - Returns 500 with 'MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID manquant' message (PASSED - expected behavior)
D. GET /api/calendar/sync/status/{appointment_id} - Returns multi-provider format {google: {synced, has_connection}, outlook: {synced, has_connection}} (PASSED)
E. DELETE /api/calendar/connections/outlook - Returns 404 when no connection exists (PASSED - expected)
F. DELETE /api/calendar/connections/google - Returns 404 when no connection exists (PASSED - expected)
G. POST /api/calendar/sync/appointment/{id}?provider=google - Returns 400 when not connected (PASSED)
H. POST /api/calendar/sync/appointment/{id}?provider=outlook - Returns 400 when not connected (PASSED)
I. Integrations page - Shows Google Calendar card with Connect button (PASSED)
J. Integrations page - Shows Outlook/Microsoft 365 card with Connect button (PASSED)
K. AppointmentDetail page - Shows ICS download button (PASSED)
L. AppointmentDetail page - Google/Outlook sync buttons hidden when no connection (PASSED - correct behavior)
M. Outlook Connect button click - Shows error toast 'MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID manquant' (PASSED)
N. Dashboard - No regression, appointments display correctly (PASSED)
O. Settings page - Integrations link accessible (PASSED)
Q. Login - Works with test credentials (PASSED)
seed_data_creation: Created test appointment 'Test Calendar Integration' (ID: c61e3194-0f4e-4ae6-8942-3dfa57893d37) for testing AppointmentDetail page
context_for_next_testing_agent: Outlook Calendar integration backend is complete but cannot be fully tested end-to-end without MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID/SECRET credentials. The API correctly returns 500 with appropriate error message when credentials are missing. Google Calendar OAuth is configured and working. Test user outlook_test@nlyt.app has workspace 'Test Workspace Outlook' and appointment 'Test Calendar Integration'. Multi-provider sync status format verified: {google: {synced: bool, has_connection: bool}, outlook: {synced: bool, has_connection: bool}}

mocked_apis.note: No APIs are mocked. Outlook OAuth flow cannot be fully tested because MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID/SECRET are not yet provided.

verified_features.Integrations_page: Both Google Calendar and Outlook/Microsoft 365 cards displayed with Connect buttons
verified_features.AppointmentDetail_sync_buttons: ICS button always visible; Google/Outlook sync buttons only visible when user has respective calendar connected
verified_features.Outlook_connect_error: Returns 500 with 'MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID manquant' when credentials not configured
verified_features.Google_connect: Returns valid authorization_url for OAuth flow
